  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Line and load conductors are reversed on the GFCI receptacle, preventing downstream protection and potentially leaving the receptacle face unprotected depending on the OEM design."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the circuit at the breaker panel, lock out/tag out, verify the circuit is dead, and correctly wire the incoming hot/neutral to the LINE terminals and downstream conductors to the LOAD terminals."
  ],
  "rationale": "A line/load reversal on a GFCI receptacle is a critical installation failure. Modern GFCIs are designed to deny power to the face or fail to reset if miswired, but older or specific models may still energize the face without providing ground-fault protection, creating a severe safety hazard.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 210.8",
    "NEC 110.3(B)"
  ]
}
